title: Employment discrimination based on sexual orientation or gender identity
subtitle: Includes discrimination in hiring, promotion, termination and harassment at work. Only legal status considered, not enforcement.
caption:
Employment discrimination based on sexual orientation or gender identity

Includes discrimination in hiring, promotion, termination and harassment at work. Only legal status considered, not enforcement.

The 2025 world map displays the legal status of employment discrimination protections based on sexual orientation or gender identity across various countries. The legend categorizes nations into seven distinct groups based on the scope of their laws. A large portion of the Americas, including the United States, Canada, Brazil, and Argentina, along with Australia and most of Western Europe, is colored blue, indicating protections for both sexual orientation and gender identity. In contrast, a vast contiguous area covering Russia, China, the Middle East, and the majority of Africa is shaded red, signifying no legal protections. Green areas, representing protections for gender identity only, appear in countries such as India, Paraguay, and parts of Southern Africa. Light yellow regions, denoting protections for sexual orientation only, include nations like Mexico, Colombia, and the Philippines. Several countries in Southeast Asia and the Caribbean are colored orange, indicating that legal status varies by region. A small number of territories are marked in grey to show that no data is available. The map visually segregates the globe into regions with comprehensive legal coverage and those with no statutory safeguards against workplace discrimination based on these specific identities.
